title: Hieracium karaulanum
intro: Hieracium karaulanum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), commonly known as a hawkweed. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium karaulanum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hieracium karaulanum is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Hieracium karaulanum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), specifically belonging to the genus Hieracium and the subgenus Pilosella. It is a type of hawkweed, which is a common name for many plants in this genus due to their association with the appearance of hawks.
